elements: 5 camera shapes represented as flat arrows with labeled positions (1 to 5)

interactions.teaches: Orbital rotation around the scene allows exploring the spatial relationship between camera positions and focus points.

interactions.label: Position
interactions.teaches: Adjusting this slider highlights the active camera and demonstrates how moving forward affects the camera's position relative to static focus points.

demo_steps.title: Initial camera position
demo_steps.narration: Observe the first camera position, represented by a simple flat arrow. Its focus point is clearly aligned directly ahead, showing the concept of forward direction.

demo_steps.title: Forward translation of the camera
demo_steps.narration: Toggle along the positions using the slider to see each subsequent camera along the path. As each camera moves forward, notice how the focus point remains constant, emphasizing the translation effect.

demo_steps.title: Exploring camera position changes
demo_steps.narration: Set the slider to the final position. Here, the fifth camera is fully forward along the path. This demonstrates the camera's potential to move without changing its focus, common in cinematic techniques where the subject remains central.

plan.camera_suggestion: Initial view slightly above ground level, looking along the forward translation path, aligning initial and final camera positions comfortably within the scene.

elements: camera representation with lens and body
central focus point on the camera axis
red arrow representing camera translation along the Z-axis
focus of expansion marked on the grids

interactions.teaches: Dragging the camera along the Z-axis simulates forward motion, showing the expansion of the motion field originating from the focus of expansion.

demo_steps.title: Initial camera setup
demo_steps.narration: This view represents the camera pointing straight toward a planar surface. The red arrow indicates forward motion along the Z-axis. The center grid is the focus of expansion where observed motion is minimal.

demo_steps.title: Simulate forward motion
demo_steps.narration: As the camera moves forward along the Z-axis, observe how the grid lines spread out. The focus of expansion remains stationary, illustrating how objects around the periphery move faster.

demo_steps.title: Focus of expansion
demo_steps.narration: Notice that the focus of expansion represents a point with minimal apparent motion. This demonstrates spatial depth as peripheral points expand outward at increasing speeds.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Frontal view of the camera and expanding grid planes, ensuring visibility of both the focus of expansion and peripheral grid movements as the camera moves forward.

elements: camera body with lens in the center
focus of expansion marked with a label on the central plane
forward translation represented by a red arrow along the Z-axis
expanding 3D grid planes indicating the motion field
annotations for focus of expansion and camera direction
peripheral points on the grid with directional motion arrows

interactions.teaches: Dragging simulates the camera's forward motion, expanding the peripheral motion field from the central focus of expansion.

demo_steps.title: Initial Setup
demo_steps.narration: At rest, the camera is centered on a planar surface. The red arrow along the Z-axis outlines potential forward motion. The focus of expansion is marked, showing minimum motion at the center.

demo_steps.title: Observe Forward Translation
demo_steps.narration: As the camera moves forward, the surrounding grid expands. Peripheral points like walls appear to move faster compared to the center. This demonstrates the parallax effect.

demo_steps.title: Focus of Expansion Dynamics
demo_steps.narration: Notice that the focus of expansion shows no movement despite the outward expansion of peripheral grids. This highlights how objects directly in the camera's path are perceived.

plan.camera_suggestion: Positioned directly facing the camera and grid planes, capturing the full motion field expansion as the camera moves forward.

plan.notes: Consider using a perspective camera in Three.js to accurately render spatial depth with expanding grid planes. Ensure the focus of expansion is clearly marked and remains central to illustrate minimal motion effect.
